faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth problem in my Oakwood home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small irregular holes in wool, silk, or cashmere items, silken tubes or cases in closets, adult moths flying near wardrobes, and larvae or webbing on stored fabrics. You may also notice damaged carpets, upholstery, or other natural fiber materials. Our Oakwood technicians can perform a thorough inspection to confirm the infestation and assess the extent of damage.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control cost in Oakwood, Staten Island?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control in Oakwood typically ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of infestation, size of your home, and number of treatments required. Minor infestations in single rooms cost less, while whole-home treatments or severe cases requiring multiple visits and specialized heat treatments fall at the higher end. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes for all Oakwood residents.

faqs.question: How quickly can you eliminate clothes moths from my Oakwood home?
faqs.answer: Most clothes moth infestations in Oakwood require 2-3 treatments spaced several weeks apart to eliminate all life stages including eggs, larvae, and adults. You'll typically see significant reduction after the first treatment, but complete elimination takes 6-8 weeks to ensure we've broken the reproductive cycle. We provide follow-up inspections to guarantee your Oakwood home remains moth-free.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning to my Oakwood home?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regularly cleaning and vacuuming closets, storing seasonal clothing in airtight containers, dry cleaning woolens before storage, using cedar blocks or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, and maintaining low humidity levels. Our Oakwood team provides detailed prevention strategies specific to your home and can set up ongoing monitoring to catch any new activity early before it becomes a full infestation.
